user_statement: Everyone on TikTok is saying this new coin is going to the moon by Friday! I'm putting all my savings in it.

copilot_humanised_response.empathetic_opening: It's super exciting to see an asset rally, and our brains naturally panic thinking we're missing out on a golden chance.
copilot_humanised_response.metric_translation: However, placing 100% of your capital into a highly volatile meme coin is like standing directly inside a roaring fire. It looks warm, but a sudden shift in wind can wipe you out instantly.
copilot_humanised_response.educational_lesson: Diversification is your shield. Keeping even 50% in broad indexes ensures that if this speculative coin plunges, your financial future doesn't plunge with it.

user_statement: My stock portfolio dropped 15% this morning. I can't take this stress! I need to buy highly volatile options to make this money back immediately, or I'm selling everything.

copilot_humanised_response.empathetic_opening: Seeing your hard-earned savings drop feels like a physical punch. Our minds are hardwired to do anything to stop that pain, leading us to take even bigger risks to 'get it back'.
copilot_humanised_response.metric_translation: Your current portfolio volatility is estimated at 42%. This means sudden swings are expected. Double-down trading in high-beta options during a correction is like speeding in heavy rain: high danger, very little progress.
copilot_humanised_response.educational_lesson: Market corrections are normal. Panic selling locks in those paper losses forever. Let's take a deep breath and look at how broad indexes have historically bounced back over time.

user_statement: This new stock trading strategy I found is guaranteed and risk-free. I've won three trades in a row, I'm 100% sure I can't lose on the next one.

copilot_humanised_response.empathetic_opening: Congrats on the winning streak! It feels amazing when your trades hit. But believing a strategy is '100% risk-free' is the most common cognitive trap in investing.
copilot_humanised_response.metric_translation: Your portfolio is split 50/50 in concentrated equities. This is like driving without a seatbelt because you're a great driver: it works perfectly until someone else hits you from the side.
copilot_humanised_response.educational_lesson: Unforeseen events happen. A safe, secure portfolio uses broad index allocations (like VOO or SPY) to cushion the unpredictable days. Let's research historical market shocks.
